repo_prior_art.cite: none; doc/API.md section 3 and the closest banked dose-response/principal-strata entries do not contain a Lee-Liu sufficient-set denominator-coupled Frechet-selector program

published_prior_art.cite: LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee Remark 3 says the conditional sufficient-set sharp bound is out of scope and gives a likely non-sharp implementable bound; Dinkelbach1967FractionalProgramming supplies only generic fractional-program machinery

repo_prior_art.cite: none; no in-catalogue qid states a common-threshold collapse certificate for pointwise Frechet plug-in sharpness

published_prior_art.cite: JonzonGabrielSjolanderSachs2025CovariatesBounds separates pointwise from uniform sharpness but does not derive this Lee-Liu sufficient-set threshold criterion
LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee identifies the sufficient-value case and its trimmed selected-outcome functional

ColangeloLee2025ContinuousDML gives kernel-localized DML for point-identified ADRFs and Semenova2024GeneralizedLee gives Lee-bound support-function inference, but neither covers this denominator-coupled selector root
FangSantos2019DirectionalInference gives generic directional-delta/resampling theory, not this kernel-localized Lee-Dinkelbach endpoint map

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:interior-curvature
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The regular inference result is conditional on a novel smooth-root interior-curvature submodel and does not claim coverage at selector ties or Frechet-boundary optima.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Gaussian Wald inference is assessed only on laws satisfying ass:interior-curvature; boundary-uniform coverage remains the OEQ.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / ass:band-estimability and ass:trimmed-numerator-estimability
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The endpoint CLT is conditional on high-level L2 estimability of the Frechet-band endpoints and trimmed numerator process; those nuisance rates are not claimed to follow from Lee-Liu or Semenova.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The regular endpoint expansion is a conditional high-level nuisance-rate claim, not a theorem deriving first-stage rates from primitive smoothness classes.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:regular-dr-endpoint / finite-bandwidth localization
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The estimator is centered through an explicit finite-bandwidth bias term and reaches the displayed Gaussian law only under local dose smoothness and undersmoothing.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The Wald claim is bound to the smooth finite-bandwidth regime where sup_lambda |B_{sigma,lambda,n}(P)|=O(h_n^2) and sqrt(n h_n) h_n^2 -> 0.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a two- or three-covariate finite-X exhibit computing H_sigma, the Dinkelbach root, and a strict gap from the pointwise Frechet plug-in when C_sigma fails.

Anchor the LeeLiu2025ContinuousLee gap in the proposal prose to Remark 3 in Section 6, where the paper explicitly says the conditional sufficient-set sharp bound is out of scope and gives a likely non-sharp bound.
State a boundary corollary for the sufficient-value reduction showing that the Dinkelbach root equals the known Lee-Liu ratio when underline_pi_d=overline_pi_d.

For the regular endpoint proposition, spell out the quantile-index range covered by local-dose smoothness for both lower and upper trimmed tails, so the O(h_n^2) bias condition visibly covers all active selector values.

recommended_next_step: Advance this field-tier angle to Stage 0; keep the accepted caveats attached so Stage 0 treats regular inference as conditional on the smooth-root and high-level nuisance-rate regime.
